km.Castlemartin, Kilcullen, Kildare, Irelan: ខាstឡិម៉ាទីន, កូuនtយ គីលដារe, អៀរឡង់

zh.Castlemartin, Kilcullen, Kildare, Irelan: 愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達卡斯特勒馬丁
zh.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustace是Rowland FitzEustace的兒子，於1310年之前出生。他至少有1個兒子。他於1330年住在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達卡斯特勒馬丁。他於1363年被列為baron of narragh。他去世於1363年。

zh-hans.Castlemartin, Kilcullen, Kildare, Irelan: 爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達卡斯特勒馬丁
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustace是Rowland FitzEustace的儿子，于1310年之前出生。他至少有1个儿子。他于1330年住在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達卡斯特勒馬丁。他于1363年被列为baron of narragh。他去世于1363年。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Eustace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Eustace (male and female), via Old French Eustace (male) from Latin Eustachius, Eustacius, from Greek Eustachios, composed of the elements eu ‘good’ and stachys ‘ear (of grain)’, hence ‘fruitful’. The name was borne by various early Christian saints, but little is known of the most famous Saint Eustace, patron saint of hunters, said to have been converted by the vision of a crucifix between the antlers of a hunted stag. In North America, this surname form of the name may have absorbed Greek surnames based on the similar personal name Efstathios, such as Efstathiadis and Efstathiou . See also Stacey .

variants.name: Heustis
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of   Eustace  .

variants.name: Estacio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Manuel, Adolfo, Aida, Benigno, Carolina, Celedonio, Cosme, Edgardo, Emilio, Enrique.
Portuguese, Galician, and Spanish: variant of Eustacio (see Eustace ).

variants.name: Eustice
meanings: English (Cornwall): variant of   Eustace  .

variants.name: Eustache
meanings: French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Eustache, from Latin Eustachius (see Eustace ). This surname was brought to the US mainly from Haiti.

variants.name: Astacio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Miguel, Jorge, Julio, Justo, Luis, Aida, Alberto, Andres, Angel, Carlos, Domingo.

Spanish: probably from a variant of the personal name Estacio (Latin Eustachius; see Eustace ). This surname is found mainly in the Dominican Republic. In Spain, it is concentrated in Alcalá de Guadaira, in Seville province.

variants.name: Eustaquio
meanings: Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Eustaquio (see Eustace ).

variants.name: Eustis
meanings: English (Glamorgan): variant of   Eustace  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Efstathiou
meanings: Greek: patronymic from the genitive case of the personal name Efstathios, classical Greek Eustathios ‘well-grounded, firm, steadfast’ (from eu ‘well’ + stathēs ‘standing’, a derivative of histēmi ‘to stand’). Eustathios was the name of several saints and patriarchs of the Eastern Church. In the early Middle Ages this name became confused in the Western Church with Eustachios ‘fruitful’ (see Eustace ).

variants.name: Huestis
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of English   Eustace  . Compare   Heustis  .

nameMeanings.name: Oliver
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a French name, Olivier, recorded as the name of one of Charlemagne's paladins (retainers), the close companion in arms of Roland in the Chanson de Roland. Whereas Roland is headstrong and rash, Oliver is thoughtful and cautious. Ostensibly this name derives from Late Latin olivarius ‘olive tree’ ( compare Olive ), but Charlemagne's other paladins all bear solidly Germanic names, so it is more probably an altered form of a Germanic name, perhaps distantly connected with Old Norse Óleifr ‘ancestral relic’. It has remained in more or less continuous use since the medieval period, becoming ever more popular since the 1980s.

en.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustace was born before 1310 as the son of Rowland FitzEustace. He had at least 1 son. He lived in Castlemartin, County Kildare, Ireland in 1330. In 1363, his occupation is listed as baron of narragh. He died in 1363.

fr.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustace est né avant 1310, fils de Rowland FitzEustace. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il a vécu à Castlemartin, County Kildare, Irlande en 1330. En 1363, sa profession de son indiquée est baron of narragh. Il est décédé en 1363.

de.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustace wurde vor 1310, als Sohn von Rowland FitzEustace geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. 1330 hat er in Castlemartin, County Kildare, Irland gelebt. 1363 ist sein Beruf als baron of narragh aufgeführt. Er starb 1363.

it.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustace è nato il prima del 1310 come figlio di Rowland FitzEustace. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Vissuto a Castlemartin, County Kildare, Irlanda nel 1330. Nel 1363, la sua occupazione è indicata come baron of narragh. Morì nel 1363.

ja.Castlemartin, Kilcullen, Kildare, Irelan: アイルランドコウントイ キルダアカストレマルティン
ja.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustaceは1310年以前にRowland FitzEustaceの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1330年にはアイルランドコウントイ キルダアカストレマルティンに住んでいた。1363年，職業はbaron of narraghと記録されている。彼は1363年に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustace(은)는 1310년 이전 Rowland FitzEustace의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1330년도에 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 카스틀레마틴에서 살았다. 1363년, 그의 직업은 baron of narragh이었다. 그는 1363년 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustace nasceu antes 1310 filho de Rowland FitzEustace. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele viveu em Castlemartin, County Kildare, Irlanda em 1330. Em 1363, sua profissão aparece como baron of narragh. Ele faleceu em 1363.

ru.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustace родился перед 1310 г. Его отца звали Rowland FitzEustace. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он жил в местности кастлемартинь, коунти каилдар, Ирландия в 1330 году. В 1363 году он работал по профессии baron of narragh. Он умер в 1363 г.

es.lifeSummary: Oliver Fitz Eustace nació antes de 1310 como hijo de Rowland FitzEustace. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Vivió en Castlemartin, County Kildare, Irlanda en 1330. En 1363, su ocupación registrada era baron of narragh. Murió en 1363.

th.1270–Male: ปี1270–ถึงแก่กรรม
th.Castlemartin, Kilcullen, Kildare, Irelan: กัสตลเอแมร์ตอิน, คูนตี กิลดาเร, ไอร์แลนด์
